definition: The action of saving or being saved from sin, error, or evil; the act of being redeemed or making amends.
example: Redemption can sound like love in the corner: tap out, Tommy—it’s okay; I love you.

definition: Training that corrects, molds, or perfects the mental faculties or moral character; strict training.
example: Discipline is the rule of the room: we train—that’s it; not a word about anything but training.

definition: Steady persistence in a course of action in spite of difficulties, obstacles, or discouragement.
example: Perseverance refuses the mat: I’m not a quitter; I don’t tap.

definition: The action or process of forgiving or being forgiven; compassionate release from resentment.
example: Forgiveness can be a plea from the ropes: tap out—it’s okay; I love you, Tommy.

definition: Courage and resolve; strength of character; firmness of mind or spirit.
example: Grit says I’m not a quitter; I don’t tap.

definition: A group consisting of parents and children living together in a household; close relations bound by love or loyalty.
example: Family cuts deepest: you never cared for underdogs, but I was your son.

definition: The ability to endure an unpleasant or difficult process or situation without giving way.
example: Endurance decides the fight: not who hits harder, but who can take it and keep coming.

definition: Physical or emotional suffering or distress; the opposite of pleasure.
example: Pain has a dark humor: I think I liked you better when you were a drunk.

definition: A male sibling; a fellow member bound by loyalty.
example: In the cage or out of it, brother is a word that can save you or break you.

definition: To signal submission in combat sports.
example: I’m not a quitter—I don’t tap—until love makes surrender a different kind of strength.

definition: To teach a skill through practice; to prepare physically.
example: We train. That’s it—not a word about anything but the work in front of us.

definition: To stop feeling angry or resentful toward someone for an offense.
example: To forgive a father is sometimes harder than any round in the cage.

definition: A competitor thought to have little chance of winning.
example: You never had interest in underdogs—until one of them was your son.

definition: A segment of a fight; another interval of suffering and will.
example: Survive the round you are in—tomorrow’s strategy cannot help tonight’s blood.

definition: Not affected by alcohol; clear-headed and restrained.
example: A sober second chance is heavier than a drunken apology.

definition: An intense feeling of deep affection; steadfast care.
example: Love can sound like a corner man pleading: tap out—it’s okay; I love you.
